qa_pairs.question: When submitting a job through Open Composer, what information can users configure about their job, and what are the main components of the job submission page?
qa_pairs.answer: The job submission page in Open Composer is divided into job parameters on the left and the job script on the right. Users can configure resources such as the number of nodes, tasks per node, wall clock time, and output file name via the job parameters. Additionally, extra fields allow customization of the 'Script Location' (where the script will be saved and run from), 'Script Name' (the name of the job script file), 'Job Name' (how it will appear in the job queue), and the 'Cluster' (e.g., Trillium or Trillium-GPU) to which the job is submitted.

qa_pairs.question: How can users monitor their submitted jobs and access their details within the Open Composer application?
qa_pairs.answer: Users can monitor their submitted jobs by navigating to the 'History' tab in Open Composer. This tab displays a list of all submitted jobs along with their status, such as Queued, Running, Completed, or Failed. Users can filter jobs and click on various column fields for more details, including opening the job in my.SciNet via 'Job ID', reopening the job script editor via 'Application', accessing the job script's location in a file browser or terminal via 'Script Location', and viewing the submitted script via 'Script Name'.

qa_pairs.question: Which specific types of Slurm jobs are currently supported by the Open Composer application?
qa_pairs.answer: Open Composer currently supports the submission of several Slurm job types: MPI Slurm Job, OpenMP Slurm Job, Hybrid MPI/OpenMP Slurm Job, Python Slurm Job, R Slurm Job, and VASP Slurm Job.
